title: Exodus: Covenant Laws
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: covenant laws and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: covenant laws and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What did the people say in unison when Moses read the Book of the Covenant to them in Exodus 24? Answer: All that the LORD has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.
segments.correct_answer: All that the LORD has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient
acceptable_variants: we will do everything the lord has said
all that the LORD commands we will obey
we will do and be obedient

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The collection of laws in Exodus 20:22 through 23:33 that followed the Ten Commandments and governed Israel's civil and religious life

An ancient Near Eastern treaty form between a great king and a subordinate people; scholars identify the Sinai covenant as following this structure with historical prologue, stipulations, and sanctions
